canonical_question: How do I implement API rate limiting?
aliases: how to rate limit an API

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: IETF RateLimit header fields draft approaching RFC status (2024); GCRA gaining adoption in cloud-native systems (2023-2025)

constraints: Rate limiting checks MUST happen before expensive operations — never after
Distributed rate limiting requires atomic read-increment operations — naive get-then-set causes race conditions
Always return 429 with Retry-After header (RFC 6585) — omitting it causes retry storms
In-memory rate limiters only work single-instance — multi-instance requires shared storage (Redis)
Per-IP rate limiting fails behind NAT/proxies — combine with API key or user ID
